meta.title: Nasc Consulting — Building the Links that Matter
meta.description: Nasc Consulting — Building the Links that Matter. Strategic consulting by Robert Crain in Sun Prairie, Wisconsin. Stakeholder relations, strategy, communications, and public affairs.
meta.og_description: I help organizations build stronger relationships, navigate complexity, and turn strategy into action.

hero.subtitle:
"Nasc" is the Irish word for link — or connection. Given my Irish roots, it was a natural fit. But it also captures something I’ve believed for 30 years, working across broadcast journalism, energy, and with the public and private sectors: the most important work happens within the connections between people.

That’s what Nasc Consulting is built to do. Help organizations and municipalities navigate complexity, build the right relationships, and find the connections with stakeholders to help ensure projects come to fruition.

"The single thread that binds all of my experience together is this: connections and relationships matter, perhaps more than anything else."

about.label: ABOUT NASC CONSULTING
about.heading: Rob Crain
paragraphs: I spent the first 15 years of my career in broadcast news — as a photojournalist, newscast producer, and eventually running television newsrooms. Those years taught me something I’ve carried into every room since: how to listen and discern, to find the story that actually matters, and how to tell it in a way people can hear on the first pass.

The next 15 years took me into the corporate world — spanning communications, customer experience, and economic development. But some of the most meaningful work happened outside the office: on boards, in community organizations, and in volunteer roles where real relationships are built between an organization and its community.
about.quote: “That combination — both sides of the table, for more than three decades — is what I bring to Nasc Consulting.”

services.label: WHAT WE DO
services.heading: "I help organizations navigate challenges that don’t have easy answers — the ones that require the right relationships, the right message, and someone who’s been in the room before."

items.title: Stakeholder & Community Relations
items.description: Competing interests don’t always mean irreconcilable differences. Usually it means people can’t see or hear each other clearly. I help get everyone to the same table - and keep them there - by helping to identify shared interests.

items.title: Strategic Communications
items.description: Good messaging isn’t about controlling the narrative. It’s about shaping one that lands with the people who actually need to hear it. I’ve been in the newsroom asking the hard questions and in the room figuring out how to answer them. That changes how you approach the work.

items.title: Business & Economic Development
items.description: Growth doesn’t happen in a vacuum. Not only do I help connect the right people, organizations, and opportunities to make things move, I also work to build-out the right structure to support that collaboration.

items.title: Regulatory & Public Policy
items.description: Regulatory processes are rarely just about the filing. They’re about who’s in the room, what they need to hear, and whether they trust you enough to hear it. I help you build that trust — before the process starts, not after.

items.title: Organizational Strategy & Alignment
items.description: The best ideas don’t always come from the people with the biggest titles. I help organizations find the signal in the noise, build out strategy, and then coalesce around it.

items.title: Municipal & Government Services
items.description: Municipalities face a challenge most organizations don’t: delivering for constituents while managing relationships that pull in every direction. I’ve been in those rooms. I help you figure out who needs to be at the table, what they need from you, and how to move things forward without leaving people behind.

clients.label: WHO WE SERVE
clients.heading: "The work looks different depending on the client — but the approach doesn’t change. Listen first. Find what actually matters. Build the connections that move things forward."

items.title: Private Sector Organizations
items.description: Companies navigating external change - utilities, professional services firms, and organizations in between - that need someone who understands the both “business” side and the needs of the community it operates in.

items.title: Nonprofits & Associations
items.description: Mission-driven organizations that need to strengthen community ties, sharpen their message, or build deeper relationships with the people who matter most to them.

items.title: Municipalities & Local Government
items.description: Cities and counties managing stakeholder processes, public communications, and community development. I’ve been on both sides of these conversations for years.

items.title: State & Federal Agencies
items.description: Government agencies that need support on policy navigation, interagency alignment, and communicating with the public in a way that builds trust rather than erodes it.

community.label: COMMUNITY AND CIVIC LEADERSHIP
community.heading: Rooted in Wisconsin
community.quote: "I’ve always believed that the best way to understand a community is to be part of it. Over the years, that’s meant showing up — on boards, in committee rooms, and even on the sidelines of youth sports."

items.title: Business Development
items.description: Board leadership across chambers of commerce, business associations, and economic partnerships — helping organizations build strategy and strengthen ties between the business community and the public sector.

items.title: Youth Athletics
items.description: Years of coaching and volunteering in youth sports programs — building teamwork, mentoring young athletes, and supporting the organizations that make it possible.

items.title: Tourism & Hospitality
items.description: Active involvement with Destination Madison, the Madison Area Sports Commission, and regional tourism efforts — connecting visitors, venues, and local stakeholders.

items.title: Community Relations
items.description: Deep roots in community organizations and civic engagement — bringing people together around shared goals and strengthening the connections between organizations and the communities they serve.

items.title: Education
items.description: Commitment to mentorship, school board involvement, and education-focused initiatives — supporting the systems and people that shape the next generation.

items.title: Economic Development
items.description: Involvement with Forward Wisconsin, Rock County 5.0, and regional economic development efforts — helping attract investment, grow businesses, and create opportunity.

items.title: Both Sides of the Table
items.description: 15 years in the newsroom asking the hard questions. 15 more in the boardroom figuring out how to answer them. That combination — journalist and executive, questioner and strategist — changes how you see problems and how you solve them.

items.title: Every Sector, One Thread
items.description: Broadcast media, energy, economic development, public affairs, hospitality. The sectors change — but the thread that connects them doesn’t: relationships, trust, and the ability to get people aligned around what actually matters.

items.title: Rooted in the Community
items.description: I don’t just consult in Wisconsin — I’ve coached here, served on boards here, built relationships here. That kind of local credibility doesn’t come from a website. It comes from showing up, year after year.

items.title: Direct. Honest. Practical.
items.description: No jargon. No fluff. No 50-page decks that collect dust. I help you figure out what needs to happen, who needs to be involved, and how to move it forward — clearly and efficiently.

contact.heading: Let's Talk
contact.success_message: Thank you for reaching out! I'll get back to you soon.
contact.subtext:
I’ve been in those rooms. I know what it takes.

Please reach out. I look forward to hearing from you.
contact.error_message: Something went wrong. Please email me directly.
contact.headline: Whether you’re navigating a complex stakeholder challenge, trying to get competing interests to see each other clearly, or figuring out how to tell your story in a way that actually lands — I’d be glad to talk.
contact.label: GET IN TOUCH
